worked: Splitting detection (deterministic Python regex) from application (agent judgment via Edit). A blind sed would strip a `[claude@]` tag AND the useful sentence; the agent keeps substance.

Reading the existing `review-gate-stop.sh` before building the nudge. Its own comments documented that a prior volume-based Stop nudge "nagged on report batches and was removed" — directly shaped the substance-not-volume trigger design.
Tuning the detector against `--all` on the real tree caught false positives (`# Step 1:`, arrows-as-emoji) that pure reasoning missed.
didnt_work: Shell commands using `yes 42 | shuf` and process-substitution kept auto-backgrounding and getting SIGTERM'd. Fix: did the random-pick + scan in one Python script instead of shell pipelines.
gotchas: Direct Stop hook required (not via hook-orchestrator) — orchestrator pipes stdout to /dev/null so `systemMessage` can't surface.
Edit-tracking state for "files written this session" lives at `/tmp/claude-edited-files-<sid8>` (one abs path/line), written by `track-edits-session.sh`. Reused by the nudge.
bash 3.2: `"${arr[@]}"` under `set -u` errors on empty array — guard with `[ "${#arr[@]}" -gt 0 ]` first.
notes: Skill is GLOBAL and repo-agnostic now. It reads the repo's own comment rubric if present (`**/comment-style.md`, CLAUDE.md, `.claude/rules/comment*.md`), else uses the built-in voice rules.
feedback: No em dashes, minimal emoji, comments as short as possible, right altitude (not idiot / not codebase-dreamer).
Profanity acceptable (esp. user-left), never flag for removal; 0 is fine, don't add any.
Over-removal acceptable since repo is human-gated, but still avoid where easy. Hard floor (NOTE(by human)/HACK/IMPORTANT/pragmas/license) never touched.

pending: (optional) Add a `~/.claude/LOOKUP.md` / FOLDERS registry entry for the new skill + hook, so they show in the address book.
(optional) Run `/cleanup-comments` for real on the Versable working-tree changed files (4 files, ~18 findings) — user has not asked yet.
(optional, user-side) Fix the stale `[claude@]` mandate in `frontend/CLAUDE.md` "Code Comments MANDATORY" section — surfaced but not edited (project repo, agent-commit-forbidden).
(residual) detect.py self-reports 2 plan-ref findings (the example vocabulary in its own doc-comment). Below nudge threshold; acceptable. Could add a self-ignore if it ever annoys.
